_comment: ISP registry. 'format' names a layout preset defined in scripts/normalize.mjs (att, fcc-snake, fcc-camel, brightspeed, google-fiber). status: 'active' = fetched + normalized; 'ready' = file URL verified but needs special handling (xlsx / huge / vertical format) before wiring; 'todo' = file is behind a JS/address/bot gate and needs a headless browser. method: direct-csv | direct-xlsx | browser-needed | vertical-csv.
_research: ISP file URLs verified 2026-06-04 via parallel research agents. See FEASIBILITY.md and git history.

isps.name: AT&T
isps.notes: Direct CSV, mixes Mobile + Fixed; keep Fixed. mainPrice is the promo price (main_is_promo).

isps.name: Spectrum
isps.notes: Standard FCC layout. All rows Fixed. Spectrum uses PLURAL 'monthly_provider_fees'. Fees are ;-separated menus. _RESI suffix = residential (also _SMB/_ENT/_MOB).

isps.name: Cox
isps.notes: Standard FCC layout; ~1684 rows (Fixed + Wireless). Keep Fixed. URL redirects to a streamed CSV.

isps.name: WOW!
isps.notes: Standard FCC layout but connection_type is blank; 'Fixed' sits in tier_plan_name, so we filter on that. Serves Columbus OH (Andrew's market).

isps.name: Breezeline
isps.notes: Standard FCC layout; ~766 plans, all Fixed. Serves Columbus OH (Andrew's market).

isps.name: Windstream / Kinetic
isps.notes: Standard FCC layout; ~75 Kinetic plans, all Fixed. File on inetsvcs subdomain, not the main site.

isps.name: Metronet
isps.notes: Standard FCC layout; ~3393 rows, all Fixed (lots of regional variants -> dedupes down).

isps.name: Starlink
isps.notes: CamelCase variant of the FCC layout. Residential file = 1 plan. Nationwide satellite coverage.

isps.name: Brightspeed
isps.notes: Human-readable layout; ~87 Fixed plans. WARNING: file URL contains a date (01-09-2026) and will change when they update — re-discover from the label page if it 404s. Fee columns ambiguous, omitted.

isps.name: Google Fiber
isps.notes: 14 per-state CSVs on a public GCS bucket; no national file. No promos, no service-type column. Same products repeat across states -> dedupes down to a handful.

isps.name: Xfinity (Comcast)
isps.notes: READY but needs special handling: file is ~56MB / ~97k FIXED rows and the URL embeds today's date (YYYY-MM-DD, ET) before the FCC reg number 0003768165. Uses a DIFFERENT longer FCC layout (date_published, price_recurring, fee_introductory, bandwidth_*_typical_low/high, etc). Don't commit the 56MB raw to git — fetch, filter, store normalized only. Needs its own format + dated-URL builder.

isps.name: T-Mobile Home Internet
isps.notes: READY but is an XLSX (needs a spreadsheet reader) and crams fees/speeds into combined text fields (additionalChargesFeesTypes, speedsProvided). No fixed/mobile column — filter by planName containing 'Home Internet'. Nationwide fixed-wireless coverage = high value.

isps.name: Optimum (Altice)
isps.notes: READY but XLSX with a custom layout (Is Introductory Rate?, Rate Card Price, Optimum Monthly Fees...). URL has a date that will change. Needs a spreadsheet reader + its own format map.

isps.name: HughesNet
isps.notes: READY but publishes ~20 per-plan CSVs in the FCC VERTICAL key-value format (not one tabular file). Needs a vertical-label parser. Nationwide satellite.

isps.name: Verizon Fios
isps.notes: Labels render client-side from an AEM SPA; no static file URL found across ~20 probes. Needs a headless browser to capture the runtime request. Covers Fios + likely 5G Home in one flow.

isps.name: Verizon 5G Home
isps.notes: Same address-gated AEM flow as Fios; may share one file. Resolve together with Verizon Fios via headless browser.

isps.name: CenturyLink / Quantum Fiber
isps.notes: Angular SPA fetches an OAuth bearer token, then GETs retrieveActiveBroadbandLabelFiles; the JSON field activeBroadbandLabelDownloadUrl[0].url is the real CSV. Replicate the token flow OR use a headless browser.

isps.name: Frontier
isps.notes: Next.js app builds the CSV client-side per product (getConsumerLabels / downloadConsumerLabels) and saves a Blob. No single static file. Needs headless browser or API replay.

isps.name: Ziply Fiber
isps.notes: No static file. Labels render from JSON APIs (GetAllProductsNutrition returns 62 products served as text/html). Could hit that JSON API directly with the right headers.

isps.name: Viasat
isps.notes: Machine-readable catalog CSV is behind a POST form with a honeypot + human-verification fragment. Needs a real browser session. Nationwide satellite.

isps.name: Mediacom
isps.notes: Angular SPA requires ZIP/address + reCAPTCHA, then an authenticated API GET getBroadbandLabelsCsv (direct hit returns 401). Needs headless browser.

isps.name: Sparklight (Cable One)
isps.notes: Entire domain sits behind a Vercel Security Checkpoint JS challenge (429 to curl). Needs a real browser to pass the challenge.

isps.name: Astound Broadband
isps.notes: Labels gated behind an address/region JS selector; soft-404s on guessed file paths. Needs headless browser. (RCN/Wave/Grande/enTouch brands.)

isps.name: Rise Broadband
isps.notes: Aggressive bot wall (HTTP 202 JS-challenge on all dynamic URLs). Labels appear at point-of-sale behind the gate. Needs headless browser; lowest priority (smaller fixed-wireless footprint).
